what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment record that links a design-source channel entry to an image asset (recent-pitches/p-28) and stores minimal metadata: id, channel, attachment_type, target URI, and added timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
standouts: target uses an image:// URI scheme pointing to a channel-local asset
channel is design-source, tying it to a pitch reference (p-28)
the record is purely referential and contains no embedded image or caption
conventions: URI-style target referencing an asset location
concerns: target URI cannot be resolved or validated from this file alone
no alt text, caption, or MIME type for the image
empty body provides no additional context
notes: A minimal image-attachment ledger entry that ties design-source pitch p-28 to an image asset and timestamps the attachment.
questions: What is pitch p-28 and where is its primary record?
What image format and dimensions does the target asset have?
Who added this attachment and was it reviewed or approved?
Is image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-28 a stable, versioned canonical reference?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended as an attachment index entry; it matches that purpose, though it carries only identifiers and no descriptive content.
intention_signals: It seems intended to record that an image attachment belongs to a specific design-source pitch, making the asset discoverable from the channel ledger without embedding the image.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme to address a channel-local asset rather than a filesystem or HTTP URL.
contribution: Provides a lightweight, timestamped attachment reference that connects a channel entry to an image asset without embedding the image itself.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-a8016f53 linking the design-source channel to image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-28, created 2026-06-22.
intention_for_embedding: To register an image attachment for design-source pitch p-28 in the channel memory ledger.
